steps.title: Apply Zero Product Property to the factored equation
steps.content:
The equation $-5(v - 8)(v - 2)(v + 9) = 0$ is already factored as a product. By the Zero Product Property, if a product equals zero, at least one factor must equal zero.

The constant factor $-5$ is never zero, so the solutions come from the binomial factors:
  • $v - 8 = 0$ gives $v = 8$
  • $v - 2 = 0$ gives $v = 2$
  • $v + 9 = 0$ gives $v = -9$

steps.title: Identify which solution matches the choices
steps.content:
The solutions are $v = 8$, $v = 2$, and $v = -9$. Checking the options:
  • Choice A: $-5$ — not a solution
  • Choice B: $-2$ — not a solution
  • Choice C: \$8$ — this IS a solution
  • Choice D: \$9$ — not a solution (we have $-9$, not \$9$)
That matches choice C — C is correct.
